title: Gentianella benedictae
taxonomy: Gentianella benedictae is classified as a species within the genus Gentianella, which is a part of the family Gentianaceae. This family includes approximately 90 genera and over 1,300 species of flowering plants. Gentianella benedictae is further categorized as a member of the Gentianella sect. Pitydonta. The genus Gentianella is native to the Americas and Eurasia. It is also known to be closely related to the genus Gentiana.

intro: Gentianella benedictae, also known as Benedict's gentian, is a perennial plant species in the gentian family (Gentianaceae) native to the high-altitude meadows of the Himalayas. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The bioactive constituents of Gentianella benedictae include iridoid glycosides, such as gentiopicroside and swertiamarin, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional medicine, Benedict's gentian is often used to prepare herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions to treat various ailments, including digestive issues and respiratory problems.
